Humanoid Locomotion
with PPO

Trained a 17-DOF humanoid agent to walk from scratch using Proximal Policy Optimization — the same RL algorithm family used in cutting-edge humanoid robotics research.

Agent in Action
MuJoCo · PPO · 17-DOF
Reward 605 Var 0.936

// Trained humanoid agent demonstrating learned gait and balance after 1M timesteps of PPO training

Capabilities
Key Features
▶ Zero-Knowledge Bootstrap

The agent starts with no prior knowledge of physics or locomotion. Every balance correction and gait pattern is discovered purely through trial-and-error interaction with the MuJoCo environment.

▶ 17-DOF Full Body Control

Controls all 17 degrees of freedom simultaneously — hips, knees, ankles, torso, and arms — learning coordinated joint movements that produce stable forward locomotion.

▶ Proximal Policy Optimization

Uses PPO — the same RL algorithm family behind OpenAI Five and modern humanoid robotics research — with clipped surrogate objective to ensure stable, monotonic policy improvement.

▶ Real-Time TensorBoard Monitoring

Full training visibility via TensorBoard — reward curves, value loss, entropy coefficient, and explained variance tracked across all 1M timesteps to diagnose and guide learning.

▶ High Explained Variance (0.936)

Achieved 0.936 explained variance — indicating the value network accurately models future reward, a key signal that the policy has learned a robust and generalizable locomotion strategy.

How It Works
Technical Implementation
[01]
Environment Setup MuJoCo Humanoid-v4 environment via Gymnasium. 17-DOF agent with full body physics simulation including contact forces, joint limits, and gravity.
[02]
Policy Architecture Multi-layer perceptron policy network trained with Stable-Baselines3 PPO. Clipped surrogate objective with entropy bonus to encourage exploration in early training.
[03]
Training Loop 1,000,000 timesteps of on-policy rollout collection, advantage estimation via GAE, and minibatch gradient updates. ~5 minutes total training time.
[04]
Reward Engineering Composite reward: forward velocity bonus, survival reward, energy penalty for excessive joint torques, and upright posture incentive to encourage natural gait.
[05]
Evaluation & Visualization Trained policy rendered in real-time MuJoCo viewer. TensorBoard logs track convergence. Final reward of 605 with explained variance 0.936 confirms successful learning.
Training Results

Tech Stack
PPO MuJoCo Gymnasium Stable-Baselines3 TensorBoard Python NumPy PyTorch
System Architecture
01
Simulation Layer MuJoCo physics engine with Humanoid-v4 model — contact forces, joint limits, full rigid body dynamics
02
RL Agent (PPO) Actor-critic MLP, GAE advantage estimation, clipped surrogate loss, entropy regularization
03
Training Pipeline Stable-Baselines3 training loop, rollout buffer, minibatch updates, checkpoint saving
04
Monitoring Layer TensorBoard logging — reward, loss, variance, entropy tracked every rollout
